title: Canning Equipment and Methods
content:
CANNING

EQUIPMENT

Select jars/glasses with smooth inner surfaces and perfect-fitting tops. Use new rubber rings or self-sealing lids. Sterilize all containers and caps immediately before filling.

Open Kettle Method: For preliminary cooking of foods; for jellies, jams, conserves, preserves, butters, marmalades, pickles, relishes.

Water Bath: Large vessel with rack for free circulation of boiling water around jars. Water at least 3 inches above jar tops. Water should be near boiling when jars placed; bring to vigorous boil quickly; maintain throughout processing period. Time counted from vigorous boil. Remove jars immediately; complete seal; invert to test for leaks.

Pressure Cooker: Must have tight-fitting cover, accurate pressure gauge, safety device. Temperature 250°F at 15 lbs pressure. Shorter processing period than water bath. Cool until pressure gauge reaches zero before removing cover. Over-processing darkens food and makes mushy.

Steam Cooker: Follow manufacturer directions. Processing periods comparable to water bath. Keep water pan filled.
